data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory, but it doesn't add the file to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means the file is generated before compilation, but MSBuild's `Compile` items are already resolved by then.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group using `<ItemGroup>` within the target, after the file is written.

Let me build first to confirm the error.

arguments.description: Build the project to see the error

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d0db22f9a6b4472b8817bddcb370b07a && dotnet build 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build the project to see the error", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content:
The file is generated but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the Compile item group so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
Confirmed. The file is generated (`Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it because it's not added to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items. This needs to happen after the file is written and before `CoreCompile` processes the items.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the Compile item group so the compiler picks it up -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never told MSBuild to compile it. By the time `CoreCompile` runs, the `Compile` item list is already frozen from the initial glob.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="..."/>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. The app now builds and runs correctly, displaying the generated build info.
